title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast examines whether AI systems can be socially engineered like humans and whether AI agents are more vulnerable to manipulation than people. Discussion covers prompt injection attacks on social platforms, self-replicating AI worms, and the security risks posed by unaudited nonhuman identities (API keys, service accounts) in enterprise environments.
writeup: This IBM Technology podcast episode examines whether AI systems can be socially engineered the way humans are, and whether AI agents prove more susceptible to manipulation than people. The discussion covers prompt injection attacks delivered through social platforms, self-replicating AI worms, and the security exposure created by unaudited nonhuman identities such as API keys and service accounts in enterprise settings. The central concern is that as agents act with delegated credentials, manipulation and identity sprawl become attack surfaces distinct from traditional human-targeted threats.
